free_text: The 12C(alpha,n)15O and 16O(alpha,n)19Ne reactions at
E(alpha) = 41 MeV
83-inch cyclotron
Practical limit on the neutron energy resolution is
0.5 and 1% for 5 and 25 MeV neutrons, respectively,
for a detector efficiency of approximately 0.06 and
using a 2 MeV low energy cut-off.
Approved by W. Parkinson, 2 November 2000.
Converted from data set R0028 and updated.
OS. REACTION in sub.5 revised
Self-supporting natural carbon target, constructed by
stacking 12 carbon foils, each nominally 40 microg/cm2
(the thickness as measured using an 241Am alpha gauge
was determined to be 480+-72 microg/cm2).
No information given on uncertainties.
This peak probably contains a small contribution
from the 5.18 MeV state.
Contains contibutions from states at 6.79, 6.87,
and 7.28 MeV.
Contains contibutions from states at 9.48, 9.61,
and 9.66 MeV.

Self-supporting natural carbon target, constructed by
stacking 12 carbon foils, each nominally 40 microg/cm2
(the thickness as measured using an 241Am alpha gauge
was determined to be 480+-72 microg/cm2).
Errors given are statistical uncertainties.
Data scanned from Fig. 6 in reference.
Solid molybdenum oxide (MoO3) on a thin nickel backing
used for measurements of line intensities relative to
the 4.62 MeV level in 19Ne.  These targets were
prepared by vacuum evaporation of natural molybdenum
oxide onto a 900 microg/cm2 nickel backing.
Oxygen gas cell containing 2.2 mg/cm2 oxygen used to
obtain the absolute angular distribution for the
strong 4.62 MeV level. The gas cell has a chamber 2.5
cm thick and windows of 0.0003 inch tantalum.
No information given on uncertainties.
